response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer with strong backend, high-performance, and software-craftsmanship credentials, but the role's core requirement is hands-on C++/STL development, and the resume shows only about six months of C/C++ work at National Instruments back in 2015 plus a university thesis project. The recent decade is concentrated in DLang, Node.js, and TypeScript. There is also no evidence of MQTT, message brokers, IoT, industrial automation, or IEC 62443 secure-development experience, which are central to this position.
strengths: Deep low-latency, async, high-throughput systems experience (Sociomantic)
Strong testing, TDD, CI/CD, and agile craftsmanship background
Broad full-stack and distributed-systems versatility across many domains
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months) and dated to 2015
No IoT, MQTT, message-broker, or industrial-automation experience
No evidence of IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle expertise
